Search on local host in which VMs using these volumes resides.
        Volume is attached to a VM, to avoid ocfs2 lock conflict,
        it is better to query the volume on the host that VM resides.
        z@grep -R --include='*.xml' "<source file=.*'/>" /etc/libvirt/qemuT)

_update_volumes_in_libvirt=
z!FSPool._update_volumes_in_libvirt
z$Pool %s release virt lock failed, %sz
client socket is closedz,Connection maybe reconnect, abandon to retry)	
lockAcquirerd
_release_virt_lockS
FSPool._release_virt_lockc
Acquire the pool lock in libvirt, used only by refresh operation.
        Make sure request the pool object one bye one when refreshing the pool.
        Multiple requests may block the request queue in libvirt client:
        refresh thread_1            refresh thread_2
        1.hold the pool lock
                                    2.request the pool object
        3.list all volumes
        The request in step 3 will be blocked in request queue, for request
        in step 2 cannot receive a response from libvirtd.
        :return: a pool object which is locked by current thread in libvirtd
        Z	exclusive)
channelro
Refresh %s failed, %sz
jobs runningN)
isActiver
NotSupportedOperationrL
ForbidRefreshZ
LibvirtError)
err_msgr7

FSPool._acquire_virt_lockc

        iSCSI:

                'type': 'iscsi'

                    'target_iqns': ['iqn.2000-05.com.3pardata:20220002ac01e',
